name: Chicken-Chili Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Grease a 3-quart baking dish.
2. In a medium bowl, mix together Cheddar cheese, half of the Monterey jack cheese, chicken, and onion. Set aside.
3. Dip each tortilla one at a time in the chicken broth to soften them slightly.
4. Fill each tortilla with about 1/4 cup of the chicken mixture, rolling seam-side down. Place them in the baking dish in two rows of six.
5. In a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, stir together chili, green enchilada sauce, and half of the chicken broth until bubbly.
6. Pour this sauce evenly over the filled tortillas.
7. Sprinkle the remaining Monterey jack cheese on top.
8. If making ahead, cover and let stand for up to 1 hour.
9. Bake uncovered in a preheated 350°F oven for 30 minutes or until hot and bubbly. Alternatively, if desired, freeze, thaw, and reheat covered in a preheated 300°F oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until hot.
10. Serve with some of the sour cream on top. The remaining sour cream can be served separately.
11. Optionally serve with chopped tomatoes and sliced ripe olives.
